memoryArtifacts.headline: Jane Walton - Memorialized in Geography?
memoryArtifacts.description: There is a place in the Portsmouth/Rye area (next to New Castle) still called Witch Creek and Witch Cove.  This is the area where Thomas and Jane Walford once lived, as they owned 200 acres on Sagamo …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Guy於1598年1月15日出生在英國英格蘭艾可分興費爾德時，她的父親Henry Guy23歲，母親Dorothy Lee Dorothee Weldon24歲。她於1623年之前與Thomas Walford在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1681年9月7日之前在美國新罕布夏州洛金哈姆波斯姆斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Guy于1598年1月15日出生在英国英格兰艾可分興費爾德时，她的父亲Henry Guy23岁，母亲Dorothy Lee Dorothee Weldon24岁。她于1623年之前与Thomas Walford在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1681年9月7日之前在美国新罕布什尔州洛金哈姆波斯姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Guy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from a common Old French personal name Guy, from ancient Germanic Wido, a short form of compound names based on the element wid(u) ‘wood, forest’, Latinized as Guido .
English (of Norman origin): alternatively, an occupational name for a guide, Old French gui (a derivative of gui(d)er ‘to guide’, of ancient Germanic origin).
Altered form of French   Guay  .

variants.name: Guise
meanings: English and French: habitational name from the district of France of this name, in Aisne.
French: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name based on the element wid(u) ‘wood, forest’ (see Guy 1).
Americanized form of German   Geiss  .

variants.name: Guiton
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Landry, Michel.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Wido (see Guy 1). Compare Guyton .
English: altered form of   Guyton  .

variants.name: Gionet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Euclide, Lucien, Armand, Marcel, Urbain.
French: variant of Guionnet or Guyonnet, derivatives of the personal name Guy 1 or its pet form Guyon . This surname is no longer found in France.

variants.name: Guedes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luiz, Carlos, Sergio, Alfredo, Orlando, Adriano, Ana, Carolina, Ernesto, Fernando, Geraldo.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Afonso, Agostinho, Anabela.  French Alphonse, Jean-Pierre.
Portuguese: patronymic from the medieval personal name Guede, probably derived from the ancient Germanic name Wido (see Guy 1), Latinized as Guido .
Breton (Finistère; Guédès, rarely Le Guédès): of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Guite
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Gilles, Maurille, Michel, Renaud.
French (Guité): from the ancient Germanic personal name Wido (see Guy 1). This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

English (Yorkshire): variant of Gyte, perhaps a topographic name from the River Goyt (Derbyshire), early recorded as Guyt, Guyot, and Gouyt. The Goyt valley is not far from Chelmorton in Derbyshire, where the modern surname has had a long history since at least the 17th century.

variants.name: Guedon
meanings: French (Guédon):
from an old personal name derived from the ancient Germanic name Wido (see Guy 1), Latinized as Guido .

variants.name: Guyon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gaston, Jean-Francois, Virginie.
French and English: from the Old French personal name Guyon, a pet form of Guy . Compare Dion 3, Gue , Guion , Yeon 2, Yon 3, and Young 4.

History: Jean Guyon du Buisson from Tourouvre in Orne, France, was one of the earliest settlers on the north shore of the St. Lawrence River in New France. He arrived together with his large family in 1634, and is said to be an ancestor of at least three out of four Québécois. Among the surnames borne by his descendants are his surname Guyon and its various altered forms, including Deo(n), Deyo, Dion, Yéon, Yon, and Young, and also the dit (‘also called’) names Darbonne and Dumontier , adopted as a surname by two of his grandsons, and the dit name Lemoine , adopted by another descendant. — This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America. As a Huguenot ancestor is listed Jacques Guyon, who was in NY by 1680. However, his descendants bear only an altered form of the surname, Gue .

variants.name: Guyer
meanings: English (London, of Norman origin): occupational name from Old French guyour ‘guide’ (see Guy 2).
Americanized form of German   Geyer  .
Swiss German: from a shortening of the expression gut Jahr (‘good year’) which as a greeting in rural Switzerland meant ‘I wish you a good harvest this year’.

variants.name: Guye
meanings: English (Derbyshire and Lancashire): variant of   Guy  .

variants.name: Guido
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Carmine, Armando, Gino, Mariano, Rocco, Gaetano, Pasquale, Santo, Umberto, Vito.  Spanish Jose, Guadalupe, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Pura, Alejandro, Alfonso.
Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: from the personal name Guido, an equivalent of Guy .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Guy was born on 15 January 1598, in Finchingfield, Essex, England, United Kingdom, her father, Henry Guy, was 23 and her mother, Dorothy Lee Dorothee Weldon, was 24. She married Thomas Walford before 1623, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 7 daughters. She died before 7 September 1681, in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, United States.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Guy è nata il il 15 gennaio 1598 nel Finchingfield, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Henry Guy, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Dorothy Lee Dorothee Weldon, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Walford prima del 1623, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 7 figlie. Morì prima del 7 settembre 1681, nel Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Guyが1598年1月15日にイギリスイギリスエセックスフィンチングフィールドで生まれた時，父Henry Guyは23歳，母Dorothy Lee Dorothee Weldonは24歳であった。1623年以前にイギリスでThomas Walfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1681年9月7日以前にアメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州ロクインガムポルトスモウスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Guy(이)가 1598년 1월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 핑친그필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Guy(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dorothy Lee Dorothee Weldon(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1623년 이전 영국에서 Thomas Walford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1681년 9월 7일 이전 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 록잉함 포츠모우스에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Guy nasceu em 15 de janeiro de 1598, em Finchingfield, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Henry Guy, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Dorothy Lee Dorothee Weldon, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Walford antes 1623, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 7 de setembro de 1681, em Portsmouth, Rockingham, Nova Hampshire, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Guy родилась 15 января 1598 г. в местности финчингфиелд, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Henry Guy было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Lee Dorothee Weldon, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Walford перед 1623 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 7 дочерей. Она умерла перед 7 сентябрь 1681 г. в местности порцмоут, роккингхам, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Guy nació el 15 de enero de 1598, en Finchingfield, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Henry Guy, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Dorothy Lee Dorothee Weldon, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Thomas Walford antes de 1623, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió antes de 7 de septiembre de 1681, en Portsmouth, Rockingham, Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos.
